comments.body: Is there a stack trace that might help narrow down the problem? If not, does mvn clean -X give one?

Probably OneDrive is locking some file or directory. It is a common issue in Windows because of the way files are locked when used. Try opening a command window in a target directory and build the project in another command line or IDE, then check if you get the same error.

using mvn clean -X shows that the plugin deletes files and folders one at a time. First deletes all the files in a folder and then the folder. 3.5.0 successfully deleted files but when it tried to delete a folder, it failed and the process was terminated.

Placing a development in OneDrive folder is always a bad idea. If you want to keep backups of your software create a bare GIT repository in OneDrive and then clone it it a folder outside of OneDrive where you can then edit/develop and commit & push changes to your Git repo backuped by OneDrive.

As written, your question sounds like something you should ask of the Apache Maven team. Given your own answer (which doesn't answer the why you ask, but does provide a solution/workaround), you may want to consider rephrasing your question.

answers.body:
I have found that by adding the following to the maven-clean-plugin xml in the project pom, that version 3.5.0 completes the clean process without error.

<configuration>
    <force>true</force>
</configuration>

According to the documentation force=true deletes read only files. I really don't know why this works but it does.

title: Why does maven-clean-plugin fail to delete files after update from version 3.4.0 to 3.5.0
body:
I upgraded my plugins to current versions, specifically the maven-clean-plugin from 3.4.0 to 3.5.0, and the result was that any attempt to run mvn clean failed with a message that authorization to delete the files failed. Nothing else changed, just the version. I only experienced this problem for projects stored on OneDrive and only after the "target" folder was synchronized to the OneDrive Cloud. With synchronization turned off, 3.5.0 worked fine. With the project on a local drive 3.5.0 worked fine. With 3.4.0 everything worked fine.

PS C:\Users\myid\OneDrive\temp\demo> & mvn clean -f "c:\Users\myid\OneDrive\temp\demo\pom.xml"
[INFO] Scanning for projects...
[INFO] 
[INFO] --------------------------< com.example:demo >--------------------------
[INFO] Building demo 1.0-SNAPSHOT
[INFO]   from pom.xml
[INFO] --------------------------------[ jar ]---------------------------------
[INFO] 
[INFO] --- clean:3.5.0:clean (default-clean) @ demo ---
[INFO] Deleting c:\Users\Jerry F\OneDrive\temp\demo\target
[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------
[INFO] BUILD FAILURE
[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------
[INFO] Total time:  1.299 s
[INFO] Finished at: 2025-09-11T13:12:21-04:00
[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------
[ERROR] Failed to execute goal org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-clean-plugin:3.5.0:clean (default-clean) on project demo: Failed to clean project: Failed to delete C:\Users\Jerry F\OneDrive\temp\demo\target\classes\com\example -> [Help 1]
